message:
feat(rchd): add wait_for_worker queueing and timing breakdown support

Extends the daemon API to support worker queueing and detailed timing:

1. SelectWorker with wait option:
   - New "wait=1" or "queue=true" parameter on select-worker requests
   - When set and all workers are busy, request is queued rather than
     rejected immediately
   - ApiRequest::SelectWorker now carries wait_for_worker boolean

2. ReleaseWorker timing breakdown:
   - Release requests can now include CommandTimingBreakdown JSON body
   - Daemon reads body after headers and parses timing if present
   - Timing propagates to BuildHistory.complete_build() for analytics

3. BuildHistory.complete_build() accepts optional timing:
   - Previously hardcoded timing: None
   - Now passes through timing from release request
   - Enables per-phase duration tracking (sync_up, exec, sync_down)

This enables future work on:
- Queue-based job scheduling for busy clusters
- Detailed build performance analytics (which phase is slowest?)
- Bottleneck identification across the build pipeline

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
